developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review! We think found a bug that causes this.  Did you happen to read all the way through to the starry exit and then use either the back button (or gaze at the floating headset to restart first?  if you have more info for us, bugs@spiraloid.net.   Update coming soon.  Also, be sure you "zero" out your controller orientation by aiming straight ahead of you and holding "home" too.

-spiraloid

reviews.review_description: I'm not a big comic book fan but I'm a sucker for cool 3D and VR experiences.  This is definitely a cool VR experience that left me wanting more. It's just the first issue, but I could see this series being 5 stars if the plot and characters are developed well over future issues. At $2, it is half the cost of most comic books and well worth purchasing. I hope devs like this get enough support to keep bringing us fun, new ways to experience VR.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review man.  We think we're onto something too.  signup for our newsletter at spiraloid.net and we'll update you on our next release!

reviews.review_description:
Now storywise, naah... But you get to see objects in front of your nose and in such detail. This is probably the first time I have ever tried to grab something. That alone, the perfect 3D models is what I liked and is worth something. There is a story too. But the models, man...
Don't use it on a note 4. It's an instant overheater. The S8+ however works fine though and stays cool.
To be clear: I've been looking since the first developer kit.
What I don't like: the models are perfect. But I can't walk around them or turn them around myself. Story reading on a vr set might be ok if you are not sweating like I do.

developer_response.body: You can turn them now with the trigger on a gear controller!

reviews.review_description:
One of the best apps for the Gear.
The graphics are gorgeous and the storyline seems pretty interesting, looking forward to the next issue!.
Really great job. Well done... Looking forward to seeing more!
The story telling and detail in the models is staggering, highly recommended
Looks like this one's awesome but right now you can't advance past 1st section as controller not working, specifically can't turn pages

developer_response.body:
Hey thanks for the review! We just updated last night with a version that should help.

Just in case, here's some info that might help:  once you find your first bonus (cellphone panels) the book displays a large green hint and deactivates all other input until you enter the bonus inside the panel (gaze at one spot on the panel until it starts sparking) If you are trying to flip pages without doing this,  the book actually ignores all input until you complete the panel bonus training.

Also check you've updated the app and have full batteries for your gear controller.  If you're still having issues, can you give us any  info about what you did right  before it got stuck and we work on getting you sorted. bugs@spiraloid.net  Thanks for giving our VR comic a go!

reviews.review_description: Great graphics and a really intriguing concept for a new generation of graphic novels. More please!
I can't turn the pages using Samsung VR controller..

developer_response.body: Hey sorry to hear that. You might want to update/reinstall because we fixed a bug last week where some people were getting stuck on the grip to flip hint.  If that doesn't work, can you tell us the exact issue that you are having?  We'd love to help.   bugs@spiraloid.net

reviews.review_description:
I'd rate it 4.5 stars, actually. There are some awesome graphics, concepts and (at least) one minigame in this issue, but the story is short and the interface with the Oculus Go controller is a bit fickle:  almost half the time a page would turn the opposite way I intended, or the book would move when I didn't want it and vice-versa.
Despite those few issues, don't miss this issue!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review, we will take a look!

reviews.review_title: Very well done VR comic app
reviews.review_description: Everything about this experience is well polished: the navigation is demonstrated well, the graphics are top notch, sound is immersive, etc. The story is interesting too, although  we don't find out much in Issue 1. I'll be back for more once it's available!.

reviews.review_title: Great artwork. It's awsome, but short
reviews.review_description:
I'm very surprised to show the wonderful graphic effects in VR. It's awsome. 
But it's been bored quickly. Cause there is no sound effect and the Issue one is too short.
Is there a bug? Diving in the tree, I can't see anything.

developer_response.body: Hmm,  there is ambient sound.  Perhaps your volume was too low?  we shall investigate.  Ah, you are right, the tree bonus broke!  we are fixing.  Thanks!

reviews.review_description: This a modern comic book and I am glad that purchase it, I have seen all figurines when I stare at a box with light blue outline.  I gave 5 stars for the graphics and style.
So innovative. Comic Experience in VR at it's best! Love it!
High quality very well done, but just simply too short for the money! would not have bought if i had known.

developer_response.body: Hey BastiaanTalsma,  Sorry to hear you thought it was too short for the money!  thanks for the note regardless.   (btw, did you find the little ball toss game on the last page?)

reviews.review_description: This game is AWESOME. You interact in some ocassions with the scene of the comic book. Really immersive whe  you dive into the picture.

Works Perfect on S8

I love how detailed the 3D models are when you go into a page.

Looking forward to the 2nd part

One of the most amazing things ive ever experienced on the vr ; l thought  it was genius  can't  wait for the rest ,,, this concept  would awesome if you could look at album covers like this and then submerge  into the album's  artwork and then listen to track's  from  the records
